task_completion.description: Verify that the required waypoints have been created in BaseCamp with the correct names and symbols.
task_completion.visual_evidence: BaseCamp's left pane (Library/List) shows the 10 specified waypoints with their corresponding icons (e.g., White Buoy for BRENTON REEF WHISTLE, Danger for GULF STREAM ENTRY, Medical Facility for EMERGENCY - HALIFAX NS).

task_completion.description: Verify that the waypoints have the correct comments/descriptions added to their properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A waypoint properties dialog box is open showing the specific comment text provided in the instructions (e.g., 'Race start area...', 'Gulf Stream entry...').

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the primary route 'NEWPORT BERMUDA 2024 RHUMB' with the correct sequence of waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route 'NEWPORT BERMUDA 2024 RHUMB' is visible in the list pane, or its properties window shows the ordered list of waypoints starting with BRENTON REEF WHISTLE and ending with ST. DAVIDS HEAD FINISH.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the alternative route 'NEWPORT BERMUDA 2024 WEATHER ALT' with the correct sequence of waypoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route 'NEWPORT BERMUDA 2024 WEATHER ALT' is visible in the list pane, or its properties window shows the ordered list including 'EMERGENCY - HALIFAX NS' instead of 'GEORGES BANK SE'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final collection was exported as a GPX file to the specified Desktop location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'Newport_Bermuda_2024_PassagePlan.gpx' is visible on the Desktop or in a File Explorer window pointing to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the Garmin BaseCamp graphical interface to create the waypoints and routes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Garmin BaseCamp application open and being interacted with, rather than a text editor or terminal writing a GPX file directly.

integrity.description: The GPX file was generated via BaseCamp's export function.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export' dialog box in BaseCamp is visible in the screenshots, saving the file to the Desktop.

integrity.description: The waypoints appear on the BaseCamp map interface, confirming they were actually created in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view in BaseCamp shows the waypoint icons. Note: They will appear on land due to the synthetic coordinates provided in the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The provided coordinates are synthetic and point to land-based street addresses rather than the sea. Do not penalize the agent if the waypoints appear on land in the BaseCamp map. The evaluation strictly depends on the correct waypoint names, symbols, comments, route order, and the final GPX export. The verifier script completely ignores the actual coordinates.
